name: Sesame Corn Chips
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Stir together the corn flour, all-purpose flour, salt, and toasted sesame seeds in a large bowl or food processor. Add the oil and blend until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Gradually add the soy sauce and enough water to form a dough that holds together in a cohesive ball.
Divide the dough into 2 equal portions. Roll each portion as thin as possible, about 1/16 inch, on a floured surface or pastry cloth. Sprinkle with salt if desired and roll over the top of the dough with the rolling pin to press in the salt.
With a sharp knife, cut the dough into 2-inch squares. Prick each square 2-3 times with the tines of a fork.
Bake for 15 minutes. Turn over and continue baking another 20-25 minutes until crisp. Cool on a wire rack. These crackers will become crisp as they cool. If needed, return them to the oven for a few minutes longer before cooling again. Yield: 40-50 chips.
Variation: Try using other roasted seeds such as poppy seeds.
